elements.name: Title
texts: "So ended my pilgrimage to Lough Derg"

elements.name: Description
texts: Knox advises Protestant travellers to visit Loug Derg and see Catholic folly

elements.name: Source
texts: Pastoral Annals. By an Irish Clergyman [i.e. James S. Knox], p. 396

elements.name: Publisher
texts: R. B. Seeley and W. Burnside, London

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Digitised by Google, sponsored by Princeton Library

elements.name: Text
texts: "An hour's ride brought me to the house of the gentleman where I was to spend the night. So ended my pilgrimage to Lough Derg - a pilgrimage I recomment such persons to make, as would study Popery in one of its chief holds over the consciences of mankind, and learn to bless that benign Providence which vouchsafed to our ancestors, grace, and strength, to break her enthralling chains, and to stand fast in the liberty, with which Christ had made them free."
